description: Update sibling source-file unit-test fixtures so they no longer depend on the engine-prefix classification branch (spec G4)
steps: In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/mod.rs, edit `discover_unified_finds_issue_725_tree` (lines 122-136): remove the `touch(&root.join(".github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md"))` call at line 128 and any assertion that depends on the file appearing in `set.features`. Keep the SKILL.md fixtures and assertions intact â€” those cover the legitimate part of issue #725.
In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/mod.rs, delete the test `discover_finds_copilot_instructions_md` at lines 149-164 entirely â€” its sole purpose is to assert the deleted branch's behavior.

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/walker.rs, edit `issue_725_tree_visible_to_walker` (lines 209-223): remove the `touch(...copilot-instructions.md)` write at line 216 and the `result.files.iter().any(|p| p.ends_with("copilot-instructions.md"))` assertion at line 222. Walker-level visibility for SKILL.md paths still asserted.

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/classify.rs, delete the test `copilot_instructions_md_classified_as_instructions` at lines 122-130 entirely â€” it mirrors the unit tests in instruction.rs::tests Case C.

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/classify.rs, edit `issue_725_full_tree_dispatch` (lines 162-175): remove the `classify_at(&PathBuf::from("/repo/.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md"), &root)` call at line 172 and any related assertion. Keep SKILL.md dispatch assertions intact.

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/types.rs, edit `discovered_feature_clone_and_eq` (line 169): replace the path string `.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md` with any other neutral path (e.g. `.claude/skills/foo/SKILL.md`) â€” the value is incidental to the Clone/Eq round-trip.

In crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs, edit `lint_unified_finds_issue_725_skills_and_instructions` (lines 1583-1628): rename to `lint_unified_finds_issue_725_skills` (drop `_and_instructions`); in the helper `write_issue_725_tree` at line 1606 remove the `touch(&root.join(".github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md"))` line; drop the assertion message `"unified path must find copilot-instructions.md (the #725 lint-side fix)"` at line 1626. Keep all SKILL.md assertions.

In crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs, edit `lint_outcome_carries_scan_counts_and_dirs` (lines 1630-1644+): change the `instructions == 1` expectation to `instructions == 0` (or drop the field assertion entirely if it is no longer load-bearing for the test's intent).

Run `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` â€” all four green. The deleted branch in instruction.rs is still in place, so all lints and tests pass; this feature only removes downstream test dependencies on it.
Append a one-line entry to research/progress.txt: `<UTC timestamp> Feature 1 complete â€” sibling source-file test fixtures decoupled from engine-prefix branch.`

In tests/features/guardrails/quality.feature, delete the entire scenario `Scenario: Lint flags oversized .github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md` at lines 64-69 (six lines, including the blank separator line).

description: Update the issue-#725 e2e test fixture and the four count-based assertions (spec G6)
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/discovery/scan_report.rs and verify `format_counts` (or the equivalent function) emits a clean string when `counts.instructions == 0` â€” e.g. `"matched 3 skills"` rather than `"matched 3 skills, 0 instructions"` or a panic. If rendering is broken for the zero case, file a separate ticket; do NOT bundle the fix here. Document the verified format string in this feature's progress entry.
In crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs, edit the file-level doc comment at line 5 to drop the `.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md` mention.
In crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs, delete the tree-diagram line `\`-- copilot-instructions.md` at line 37.

In crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs, edit `build_issue_725_fixture` (lines 39-58): drop lines 55-57 (the `let instructions_path = root.join(...)` write of `.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md`), and the `// copilot-instructions.md sibling to skills/.` comment at line 55. The fixture now writes only the marketplace stub and the three SKILL.md skills.

In crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs, in `migrate_finds_issue_725_skills` (lines 64-94) update the stderr assertion at lines 78-81 from `"matched 3 skills, 1 instruction"` to the verified zero-case format string from step 1 (typically `"matched 3 skills"`).
In crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs, in `lint_summary_visible` (lines 100-127) update the stderr assertion at lines 116-119 to the same verified zero-case format string.

In crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs, in `lint_ci_github_summary_on_stderr_diagnostics_on_stdout` (lines 153-179) update the stderr assertion at lines 175-178 to the same verified zero-case format string.
Leave `lint_no_summary_suppresses_stderr_summary` (lines 133-147) unchanged â€” it asserts on the absence of `"Scanned"`, which is independent of the instructions count.

Run `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` â€” all four green. The deleted-branch behavior is still active in instruction.rs; the e2e tests now assert on the post-cut count regardless.
Append a one-line entry to research/progress.txt: `<UTC timestamp> Feature 3 complete â€” e2e fixture + count assertions updated; format_counts zero-case verified as <string>.`

description: Cut the engine-prefix classification branch in `discovery/instruction.rs`, delete its Case-C unit tests, edit the structural test, and add the load-bearing regression guard (spec G1, G2, G3, G12)
steps: In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, delete module-doc lines 11-13 (the Case 3 enumeration listing `<engine>-instructions.md` and the four filenames).

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, delete line 32 (the doc comment `Engine prefixes accepted in the <engine>-instructions.md shape.`) and line 33 (the `const ENGINE_INSTRUCTION_PREFIXES: &[&str] = &["copilot", "claude", "agents", "gemini"];` declaration).

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, edit `is_instruction_filename` at lines 66-70: remove the third disjunct `|| matches_engine_instructions(file_name_lower)` at line 69. The function body should reduce to: `INSTRUCTION_FILENAMES.contains(&file_name_lower) || file_name_lower.ends_with(".instructions.md")`.
In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, delete lines 72-79 (the `matches_engine_instructions` function and its doc comment) entirely.

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, delete the test-section comment at line 143 (`// --- Case C: <engine>-instructions.md (the #725 fix) ---`) and all Case-C tests immediately following it: `copilot_instructions_md_matches_issue_725` (145-150), `claude_instructions_md_matches` (152-155), `agents_instructions_md_matches` (157-160), `gemini_instructions_md_matches` (162-165), `engine_instructions_md_case_insensitive` (167-171).

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, delete the negative tests that exist solely to guard the deleted branch: `instructions_copilot_md_wrong_order_no_match` (180-184), `unknown_engine_prefix_no_match` (186-191), `copilot_tools_md_does_not_match` (193-197), `copilot_instructions_md_with_extra_suffix_no_match` (199-203), `just_dash_instructions_md_no_match` (210-214). Leave `random_md_does_not_match` (175-178) and `empty_filename_no_match` (205-208) alone â€” they cover the surviving branches.

In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs, edit `classify_returns_path_unchanged` (lines 218-228): replace the path `/repo/.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md` with `/repo/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (or any `INSTRUCTION_FILENAMES` filename), and replace the call `classify("copilot-instructions.md", &path, Engine::Copilot, &root)` with `classify("CLAUDE.md", &path, Engine::Claude, &root)`. The structural assertions (`feat.path == path`, `feat.source_root == root`, etc.) remain valid because the test's intent is `classify` echoes its inputs unchanged, not that any specific filename matches.

Append a new test at the end of `mod tests` in crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs named `nested_engine_instructions_md_not_classified`. Body: a one-line comment `// Regression guard: see specs/2026-05-02-engine-instructions-md-pattern-removal.md` followed by four `assert!(classify_with("<name>").is_none());` lines for `copilot-instructions.md`, `claude-instructions.md`, `agents-instructions.md`, and `gemini-instructions.md`. This is the load-bearing G12 guard against future agent-loop re-introduction.
Run `grep -rn "ENGINE_INSTRUCTION_PREFIXES\|matches_engine_instructions" crates/libaipm/src/` â€” must return zero hits (no remaining references in production code).

Run `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` â€” all four must be green with zero warnings. Confirm no .unwrap()/.expect()/panic!()/println! anywhere per CLAUDE.md lint policy.

Run the coverage gate from CLAUDE.md: `cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace`, `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch`, `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc`, then `cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs|lsp\.rs)'`. TOTAL branch column must be â‰Ą 89%.
Append a one-line entry to research/progress.txt: `<UTC timestamp> Feature 4 complete â€” engine-prefix branch removed from instruction.rs; regression guard added; coverage <pct>% / target 89%.`

description: Update CHANGELOG.md to remove the misleading bullet and add a Removed entry citing this spec (spec G8)
steps: In CHANGELOG.md, locate the bullet at line 10: `- **\`aipm lint\` now recognises \`<engine>-instructions.md\` files** â€” \`copilot-instructions.md\`, \`claude-instructions.md\`, \`agents-instructions.md\`, and \`gemini-instructions.md\` are all classified as instruction files. Closes the second silent-drop case from issue #725.` Delete this bullet entirely.
Verify the sibling bullet at line 9 (the migrate-side skill-detection fix) remains unchanged â€” that bullet is the legitimate part of issue #725 and stays.

Under `## [Unreleased]`, locate or create a `### Removed` subsection and add a single bullet: `- **\`aipm lint\` no longer classifies \`<engine>-instructions.md\` files (\`copilot-instructions.md\`, \`claude-instructions.md\`, \`agents-instructions.md\`, \`gemini-instructions.md\`) as instruction files** â€” engine-documentation verification (Anthropic Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Google Gemini CLI, AGENTS.md spec) confirmed three of the four prefixes correspond to filenames no engine reads, and the fourth (\`copilot-instructions.md\`) is recognized by GitHub Copilot only at \`.github/copilot-instructions.md\` (bare) and \`$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md\`. See specs/2026-05-02-engine-instructions-md-pattern-removal.md.`

If the `### Fixed` subsection becomes empty after the deletion, leave the subsection heading in place but add a one-line note `(no changes in this release yet)` if that is the project convention; otherwise delete the now-empty subheading. Verify against existing CHANGELOG conventions before choosing.
Run `git diff CHANGELOG.md` and confirm the diff shows exactly one bullet removed under `### Fixed` and exactly one bullet added under `### Removed`. No other changes.

Run `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` â€” all four green (CHANGELOG edits do not affect build, but verify nothing else regressed).
Append a one-line entry to research/progress.txt: `<UTC timestamp> Feature 6 complete â€” CHANGELOG updated; Removed entry added under [Unreleased].`

description: Final verification â€” workspace-wide grep for engine-prefix references; cargo gates green; branch coverage â‰Ą 89%; spec G11 satisfied
steps: Run a workspace-wide search for any remaining reference to the engine-prefix family: `grep -rn 'ENGINE_INSTRUCTION_PREFIXES\|matches_engine_instructions\|claude-instructions.md\|agents-instructions.md\|gemini-instructions.md' --include='*.rs' --include='*.md' --include='*.feature' --include='*.toml' --include='*.json' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' .` â€” all surviving hits MUST be in (a) specs/2026-05-02-engine-instructions-md-pattern-removal.md (this spec itself), (b) research/docs/2026-05-02-engine-instructions-md-pattern-removal.md (the research doc), (c) the regression test added in Feature 4, (d) the CHANGELOG `### Removed` entry from Feature 6, or (e) the withdrawal preamble in 2026-05-01-unified-discovery-... from Feature 5. Any other hit indicates a missed edit.

Run a separate search for the bare `copilot-instructions.md` token (which IS a real Copilot path): `grep -rn 'copilot-instructions.md' --include='*.rs' --include='*.md' .`. Surviving hits should be limited to (a) the regression test, (b) the spec/research/CHANGELOG entries enumerated above, (c) `.github/workflows/reverse-binary-analysis.md:142` (illustrative reference to the real `.github/copilot-instructions.md` path â€” keep), (d) `specs/2026-04-11-lint-instructions-oversized.md:36` (factual prose about Copilot CLI conventions â€” keep), (e) `research/docs/2026-03-28-copilot-cli-source-code-analysis.md` and `research/tickets/2026-04-11-185-prevent-long-instructions-files.md` (historical research â€” keep).

Run all four cargo quality gates from CLAUDE.md: `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`. All four MUST pass with zero warnings.

Run the branch-coverage gate from CLAUDE.md: `cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc && cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs|lsp\.rs)'`. The TOTAL branch column MUST be â‰Ą 89%.

Confirm the regression test from Feature 4 is present and passing by running `cargo test --workspace -p libaipm nested_engine_instructions_md_not_classified` (or equivalent) â€” output must show 1 test passed.

Append a final summary entry to research/progress.txt: `<UTC timestamp> All features complete â€” engine-prefix branch removal verified workspace-green; branch coverage <pct>% (target â‰Ą89%); regression guard active.`
